title: Two Seas That Meet but Never Mix — The Qur'an Described an Invisible Wall
_note: CINEMATIC, hook-first, 60s+. Footage = ocean/water (no humans; verified). NO recital; voice speaks English. SOURCE: Surah Ar-Rahman 55:19-20 (and Al-Furqan 25:53). SCIENCE: halocline/pycnocline density barrier between water masses (e.g. Atlantic vs Mediterranean at Gibraltar). ACCURACY GUARD: framed on the REAL invisible density barrier; the visible 'two-colour' confluences (river mouths/estuaries) are real but the barrier itself is sub-surface density — NOT the overclaimed 'oceans never touch' photo. Safe-zone, legible captions.

segments.text: There are places in the ocean where two seas meet, and refuse to fully mix. The Quran described this fourteen hundred years ago.
It said: He released the two seas, meeting together. But between them is a barrier they do not cross.
For centuries this sounded impossible. Water is water. How can two seas refuse to mix?
Sailors noticed it for generations. Two waters, side by side, with different colors and temperatures. But nobody could explain why they stayed apart.
Then oceanographers found the answer. Where seas of different saltiness and temperature meet, an invisible wall of density forms between them.
Each sea keeps its own temperature, its own saltiness, even its own sea life. Held apart by a barrier you cannot even see.
We only measured it with modern submarines and sensors, deep beneath the surface.
You cannot see it with your eyes. But it is there. Exactly where the Quran said it would be.
Between the two seas is a barrier, which they do not cross.
A wall you cannot see, named in a book thirteen centuries before we found it.

sources: Qur'an, Surah Ar-Rahman 55:19-20 (and Al-Furqan 25:53) — 'He released the two seas, meeting; between them is a barrier they do not transgress.'

Oceanography: a halocline/pycnocline is a sharp density gradient (salinity + temperature) that resists mixing between water masses; e.g. Atlantic and Mediterranean water at the Strait of Gibraltar keep distinct properties for hundreds of km (Britannica; Wikipedia).
ACCURACY: framed on the real, mostly invisible density barrier — not the overclaimed 'two oceans never touch' viral photo.
Qur'an 55:20 shown Arabic + English; not recited. All Pexels clips verified human-free.
